what_it_is: All Hampshire College parcels in Amherst and Hadley, each placemark labeled with the parcel ID. Clicking a parcel opens a balloon showing owner, zoning, land use, acres, FY assessed value, and encumbrance (creditor, silo year, instrument, book and page).
when_to_use: When you need any parcel-level information (ownership, zoning, encumbrance, value). Drop this on top of any basemap for context.
source: MassGIS Level 3 Standardized Assessors' Parcels (Amherst FY2024, Hadley FY2025) plus encumbrance lookup from the Hampshire County Registry of Deeds and Hampshire College FY2025 audit.

what_it_is: MassGIS Protected and Recreational Open Space polygons clipped to a 500-meter envelope around the Hampshire parcels. Each placemark balloon shows site name, owner type (municipal, state, federal, land trust, private nonprofit, other), protection level (permanent, limited, none), and mapped acres.
when_to_use: When you want to see what's permanently conserved around the Hampshire holdings. Useful for understanding which neighbors are off the market or which areas already form a regional green network.
source: MassGIS Protected and Recreational Open Space.

what_it_is: MassDEP regulatory wetland polygons themselves, one placemark per wetland, clipped to a 500-meter envelope around the Hampshire parcels. Each placemark balloon shows the wetland cover type (wooded swamp, shrub swamp, shallow marsh, deep marsh, open water, bog, etc.) and mapped acres.
when_to_use: When you want to see only the actual wetland edges, without any buffer. Useful for orientation and for identifying cover types per wetland.
source: MassGIS DEP Wetlands (regulatory polygons, photo-interpreted at ~1:12,000).

what_it_is: The 0 to 100 ft ring around every wetland: the Massachusetts Wetlands Protection Act Buffer Zone. Drawn as the difference between the 100 ft buffer and the wetland itself, so it occupies its own ring with no overlap.
when_to_use: When the conversation is about the standard state-WPA buffer reach. This is the regulatory boundary triggered by a Notice of Intent.
source: Derived locally by buffering DEP wetlands 100 ft outward in MA State Plane Mainland (EPSG:26986), then differencing the wetland itself.

what_it_is: The 100 to 200 ft ring beyond the WPA Buffer Zone: an additional band that approximates the Riverfront Area and a conservative reading of Amherst's local Wetlands Protection Bylaw. Drawn as the difference between the 200 ft and 100 ft buffers, so it occupies its own ring with no overlap.
when_to_use: When you want to flag the additional area covered under a conservative or strict-buffer scenario, such as Amherst's local bylaw read generously. Use alongside the 0 to 100 ft ring to show the total regulated reach.
source: Derived locally by buffering DEP wetlands 200 ft outward and differencing the 100 ft buffer.

what_it_is: A single unioned polygon: every wetland plus its 200 ft outward buffer, merged into one shape. Combines the three-layer split (actual + 0 to 100 ft ring + 100 to 200 ft ring) into one toggle for the total regulated reach.
when_to_use: When you want one toggle that shows the full regulated extent (wetland plus 200 ft) without managing three layers. Visually equivalent to turning on all three split layers together.
source: Derived locally by buffering DEP wetlands 200 ft outward and unioning.
